key_insight: IFENN couples FEM with a pre-trained neural operator to model both initiation and propagation of phase-field fracture across unseen geometries.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster offline training and generalizable fracture prediction for solid mechanics applications with reduced sampling cost.
method: DeepOKAN predicts phase-field evolution during crack initiation using physics-informed training.
A CNN handles the propagation stage after initiation.
Artificial boundary conditions suppress unrealistic far-field DeepOKAN extrapolation.
evidence_signal: The method is trained once on a benchmark geometry with only a few increments and limited Gauss-point samples, then evaluated on both trained and unseen geometries.
watch_item: Accuracy may depend on careful selection of fracture-process-zone samples and on the artificial boundary conditions used to control extrapolation.
paper_title: A hybrid IFENN solver for generalizable modeling of phase-field fracture initiation and propagation
